DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, APR-22-2026 HEARING HELD RE: PLAINTIFF PAUL REEB'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH A MODIFICATION. PLAINTIFF PAUL REEB'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO HIS FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IS DENIED AS TO INTERROGATORIES 1-3, GRANTED AS TO INTERROGATORIES 10 AND 12, AND GRANTED IN PART AS TO INTERROGATORY 13. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: S. KANE, NOT REPORTED (301/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Apr-22-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DAVID J. GALLO, Esq. of Law Offices of David J. Gallo (Tel: 858-509-3652) for plaintiff Paul Reeb;
Atty. BLAIR C. SENESI, Esq. of Littler Mendelson, P.C. (Tel: 415-433-1940) for defendant Air Education, Inc.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF PAUL REEB's Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Special Interrogatories.

Matter argued and submitted. The court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification.

Plaintiff Paul Reeb's motion to compel further responses to his first set of special interrogatories is denied as to interrogatories 1-3, granted as to interrogatories 10 and 12, and granted in part as to interrogatory 13.

Reeb's requests for information about non-California tutors (interrogatories 1 and 2) and tutoring sessions conducted by non-California tutors (interrogatory 3) are premature. Unless and until there is a ruling that the pertinent California Labor Code provisions apply to California tutors, providing this information imposes an unwarranted burden on defendant Air Education, Inc.

Reeb's requests for information about schools and school districts who have been customers or clients of Air Education (interrogatories 10 and 12) are discovery relevant and all of Air Education's objections to providing that information lack merit. No later than May 13, 2026 Air Education must serve verified code-compliant further responses to interrogatories 10 and 12 providing all responsive information.

Reed's request for contact information for officers of Air Education (interrogatory 13) is permissible as to those persons who are no longer employed by and are not agents of Air Education, but not as to those persons who are currently employed by or are agents of Air Education, as to whom it can contact through Air Education's attorneys. No later than May 13, 2026 Air Education must serve verified code-compliant further responses to interrogatory 13 providing all responsive information as to those persons who are not currently employed by and are not currently agents of Air Education.

As to interrogatory 1 and interrogatory 13, Air Education is to provide for current officers the city in which the officer resides.

The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: S. Kane, Not Reported (301/HEK)
